titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Yale Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by N. X. Rizopoulos with the assistance of Anne Willard

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday April 20, 2020 at 07:11

did.unittitle: William Yale papers
did.abstract: Correspondence, intelligence reports, and other papers of William Yale, author, diplomat and professor. The papers relate primarily to problems in the Near East during and immediately after World War I. Included are reports and agreements concerning Palestine and Syria and various reports by special commissions on Turkey, Arabia, and Zionism. There is also material relating to the Paris Peace Conference.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Yale Papers (MS 658). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in seven series: I. General Correspondence. II. Wartime Intelligence Reports. III. Miscellaneous Materials on Palestine. IV. Miscellaneous Materials on Syria. V. Miscellaneous Materials on Arabs and Arabia. VI. Miscellaneous Paris Peace Conference Materials. VII. Miscellaneous Items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To a large degree the William Yale Papers consist ofcopies of miscellaneous documents (official, semi-official and private; American as well as French, British and Middle Eastern in provenance) pertaining to the break-up and reorganization of certain segments of the old Ottoman Empire during the years 1917-1919. The bulk of the material deals with Syrian, Palestinian, Arab and Zionist affairs.* As Yale himself explained (in a letter sent to Charles Seymour in 1928, when Yale made the gift of his papers to the Yale University Library): "Some of these documents are [also to be found] in the Hoover Collection at Stanford, some are at Princeton, and most of them [are] in the files of the Department of State or the War Department. However, some of them are not in any collection." A few additional items were incorporated at a more recent date: these include Yale's report on Henry Morgenthau's secret mission to Constantinople in 1917; a letter from Jamal Husseini (written in 1962) describing a meeting organized in 1921 between Weizmann and a group of prominent Arabs to discuss Zionist demands in Palestine; and a letter addressed to Yale by the legendary T. E. Lawrence, describing in vivid detail the Arab occupation of Damascus.
* Most of the items in the collection are in English, although a good many of them are copies-in-translation of Arab or Hebrew originals.
The William Yale Papers, comprising roughly 160 folders, are divided into seven series:
This series is subdivided as follows:
(a) Reports Nos. 1-34 [including covering letters from Yale to Leland Harrison]; plus three preliminary reports [1917], one diary excerpt, and a Résumé of Nos. 1-34.
(b) Reports sent to U. S. Military Attaché, London [incomplete set; arranged chronologically]
(c) Miscellaneous official communications with Department of State, 1917 June 30 - 1918 July 19
III. Miscellaneous Materials on Palestine (1 box)
IV. Miscellaneous Materials on Syria (1 box)
V. Miscellaneous Materials on Arabs and Arabia (1 box)
VI. Miscellaneous Paris Peace Conference Materials (1 box)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Yale (a collateral descendant of Elihu Yale) was born in Dobbs Ferry, N.Y., on August 6, 1887. He attended the Lawrenceville School and received his Ph.B. degree from Yale as a member of the class of 1910-S. For the next five years Yale worked in various administrative capacities for the Standard Oil Company of New York, particularly in the Near East. He eventually became SOCONY'S chief representative in Jerusalem, where he resided continuously from June 1915 to March 1917 and where he acquired a speaking knowledge of Arabic and a thorough knowledge of French. He left Palestine in early March 1917 and proceeded to Constantinople, for the most part traveling over the Bagdad Railroad and passing through Damascus, Aleppo, Tarsus, Koniah and Eschi Seher. He returned to the United States soon after America's entry into the First World War.

For a short while afterward, Yale worked with the American Field Service in Washington, D.C. Then, in August 1917, he received an appointment as a Special Agent of the Department of State with instructions to proceed to Egypt. Yale stopped first in London, where he held consultations with William H. Buckler (Colonel House's special liaison man at the American Embassy), with members of the British Military and Admiralty Intelligence Offices, as well as with the heads of the various Middle Eastern Relief Committees. He also visited Paris, where he conferred with many Zionist and Arab leaders (including Boghos Nubar Pasha). Finally, traveling via Rome, Taranto and Malta, he arrived in Alexandria on October 19, 1917.

Yale outlined the purpose of his mission (and the peculiarities of his position in Egypt) in the very first report, which he addressed to Leland Harrison (his immediate chief at the Department of State):

"On my arrival at Cairo I presented myself to Mr. Knabenshue, Vice-Consul in charge of the Diplomatic Agency, and explained to him my presence here and mission. After a lengthy conference with Mr. Knabenshue, in which we throughly discussed my position here, we both came to the Conclusion that the only course to pursue was that he should present me to the [British] High Commissioner, Sir Reginald Wingate, as a special agent of the Department of State, sent to Egypt to keep the United States Government informed of the events of importance occuring east of Suez, in Syria, Palestine, Arabia and the Hedjaz. It was after careful consideration of the question that we came to this decision for the following reasons.

My presence in Egypt as a Special Agent of the Department of State was known to the British Authorities, as I was travelling under a special diplomatic passport defining my position. My work here necessitates my meeting and interviewing the leaders of the groups interested in Palestine and Syria, viz: Zionists and Anti-Zionists, Moslem Arabs and the various sects of Christian Arabs and all other interests. Such activities on my part would have been carefully watched and followed by the British, whose secret agents are everywhere. My comings and goings would have been fully reported upon, and it is very probable that even my conversations would at times have been reported verbatim to the British Authorities. Not only would the British have known the nature of my work, but they might have surmised and imputed other motives to my presence in Egypt. Not only would I personally have been under suspicion and my activities hindered and curtailed but suspicion would have been thrown upon the American [Diplomatic] Agency, which would have resulted in placing both Mr. Knabenshue and myself in a most unpleasant and disadvantageous light."
Yale sent weekly reports to Washington during the period October 1917 - July 1918, paying special attention to Syrian and Palestinian questions.

On June 20, 1918, Yale received an appointment as captain in the U. S. Army through the auspices of the Department of State. This "promotion" was obviously meant to facilitate his movements in and out of Egypt. In fact, soon thereafter Yale was also informed by the office of the U. S. military attaché in London that General Allenby, Commander in Chief of British Forces in Palestine, had agreed to receive him at his headquarters as an official American military observer. Yale jumped at this opportunity, though wondering whether henceforth the Department of State or the War Department would be at the receiving end of his intelligence reports. In any event, Yale was elated: "Although as military observer my activities will in some ways be restricted," he wrote to Harrison, "they will on the other hand be extended by my being able to go to Palestine and later to Akabah and the Hedjaz, and if necessary to Mesopotamia. In so doing I shall be able to get into touch with the leading Arabs in the various parts of the Arab world, and will in the end secure first-hand information of what is going on." In fact, Yale was thus able to observe from close quarters Allenby's brilliant final campaign (September 19 to November 11, 1918), and in his reports he commented at length upon these operations, as well as upon the varied and complicated political ramifications.

After the signing of the armistice, Yale was ordered to proceed to Paris, where he was attached to the staff of the American Commission to Negotiate Peace as an expert on Syrian and Arabian affairs. He remained in France until May 1919, when he was sent back to the Levant as technical advisor to the American section of the International Commission on Mandates in Turkey. He returned to Paris at the end of the summer, spent some more time in London in the fall, and was finally demobilized in December 1919. For a few years after his return to the United States Yale worked for a number of American firms at home and abroad. Then, in 1928, he began teaching modern European history at the University of New Hampshire, and he remained there for the better part of the next twenty-five years of his life. During World War II, Yale was employed by the Department of State as a special consultant on Middle Eastern affairs. Before his retirement Yale also taught history at Boston University. He is the author of The Near East: A Modern History (1958; revised edition 1968). Yale died in 1975.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

persname.part: Allenby, Edmund Henry Hynman Allenby, Viscount, 1861-1936

corpname.part: Paris Peace Conference. United States Territorial Section (1919-1920. United States Territorial Section) -- 1919-1920

did.unittitle: General Correspondence
Allenby, General Edmund, autograph letter to William Yale, .
Buckler, W. H., letter to Yale, [original]. With accompanying copy of Buckler's memorandum on "The Policy of the United States Relative to the Treaty with Turkey."
Husseini, Jamal, letter to Yale regarding meeting with Weizmann, . Report by Yale concerning letter, dated .
Lawrence, T. E., copy of a letter written to Yale concerning the affairs of the Turkish Hospital, Damascus, in 1918; London, .
Watson, J. K., to Yale, [original].
Westermann, W. L., copy [inc.] of Yale's letter to Westermann from Beirut, . Includes memorandum entitled "Strong National Feeling,"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Wartime Intellegence Reports
Reports 1-34 and Related Materials
Report on "Palestine - Syria Situation"
Diary notes
Preliminary reports from Paris, and from Cairo
Report 1: "The Syrian Question"
Report 2: "The Arabia and Hedjaz Situation"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 3: "British Policy in the Near East"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 4: "Syrians and Zionism"
Report 5: "Recent Developments in the Palestine Situation"
Report 6: "France and Syria"
Report 7: "Notes on Syrian Affairs for the Week Ending Dec. 10"
Report 8: "Arab Notes." Includes translation from Hebrew of a letter of introduction given by Aaronsohn to William Yale at Paris 1917 October, for his brother, Alexander Aaronsohn at Cairo, Egypt.
Report 9: "Arab Activities and the Balfour Declaration"
Report 10: "Notes on the Zionist Question"
Report 11: "Activities in Turkey re: Syrian Affairs"
Report 12: "The Syrian Question." Includes translation and French text of article by Adib Pasha on "The Future Government of Mount Lebanon."
Report 13: "British Attitude towards Turkish Propaganda"
Report 14: "The Syrian Question (Views of a certain group of Non-Annexationists)." Includes report to General Clayton, Chief of British Intelligence in Egypt, by Suleiman Nassif Bey.
Report 15: "Notes on 'The Turkish and Pan-Turkish Ideal." Includes copy of article by Tekin Alp, "The Turkish and Pan-Turkish Ideal," Constantinople.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 16: "Syria and the Syrians; a Sketch." Includes copy of "an article by a young British officer for his government": Syria, the Raw Material . Fragmentary notes written in 1918, not circulated. By T. E. L.[awrence?]
Report 17: "The Growing Discontent among the Arabs"
Report 18: "Discontent Among the Arabs"
Report 19: "Zionism and the Arab Movement." Includes copy of letter by Sir Mark Sykes to the Syrians, and translation of Adib Pash's "Historical Review of the 'Alliance Libanaise,'" Cairo.
Report 20: "The Zionist Commission"
Report 21: "Military Operations in Eastern Palestine"
Report 22: "Zionist Commission and Syrians in Egypt"
Report 23: "Views and Opinions of the Syrian Moslems in Egypt"
Report 24: "Zionism and Palestine"
Report 25: "News from Palestine"
Report 26: "The Syrians vs. the French"
Report 27: "The Question of Syria and Mt. Lebanon." Includes copy of the "Statute for the Administration of the Lebanon" (English and French texts).
Report 28: "Syrian Propaganda Relative to the United States"
Report 29: "The Syrian Mission in Palestine"
Report 30: "The Article on the Druzes by Selim Izzeddin Bey." Includes copy of "The Druzes and Recent Events in Syria," by Selim Izzeddin Bey.
Report 31: Notes on the French and British in Palestine"
Report 32: "Summary of the Operations of the Cherifian Forces during the Past Four Months." Includes addendum, "The Wahabites," by William Yale.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 33: "Arab Empire Propaganda"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 34: "The Meeting of Dr. Weizmann and Emir Faisal." Includes copy of Letter of Sd. Suleiman Nassif to the Editor of the "Al Kawab," Cairo.
Copy of Resumé of 34 Reports of Preceding Ten Months: "Political Situation of the Arab Provinces of the Ottoman Empire," Includes copy of covering letter War Dept. to Harrison.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Reports sent to U. S. Military Attaché, London
Report 61: "Recent Political Events among the Arabs"

Report 112: "The Political Situation in Syria." Includes proclamation (in French and English) re: expulsion from Jerusalem of American citizens between the ages of 17 and 50 years, by Agath, Governor of Jerusalem; also " Lissam Ul-Arab ( The Language of the Arabs ), a political and social daily."

Report 128: "The Battle of Tul Keram and Military Operations in Palestine and Syria, September 15 to October 26th," outline of report and report. Includes copy of "Official Report of General Allenby on the final military operations in Palestine and Syria, which ended in the capture of Aleppo, October 26, 1918."
Report 163: "The Question of Greek Orthodox Patriarchate at Jerusalem." Includes extract from Egyptian Gazette re: return of Greek Orthodox Patriarch to Jerusalem.
Report 173: "Facts in connection with the Syrian Question which have recently been learned"
Report 174 (1): "Great Britain, France and the Near East"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 174 (2): "The Near East and the Western World"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Report 175: "An Arab Empire" [not sent]
Report 176: "Present Situation in Palestine and Syria." Includes copy of "Petition to the United States," by Preacher at Mosque of Omar Mohammed El Afifi.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Official Communications
Miscellaneous official communications between William Yale and Leland Harrison and Robert Lansing
Miscellaneous Materials on Palestine
Copy of Col. Finley's report on the budget of the American Red Cross Commission to Palestine

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: "The Boundaries of Palestine," Copy of Zionist[?] Memorandum
Felix Frankfurter's Project of Mandate form for Palestine, [with MS: corrections by W. Y. and W. L. Westermann]
Copy of Memorandum on Property on Palestine
Copy of memorandum of suggestions for co-operation between Zionists and Palestinians
Memorandum on Weizmann - Feisal Interview, [with MS annotations by W. Y., 1927]
Copy of Memorandum on World Jewry
Copy of telegram to Balfour from Syrian Community in Cairo
Copy of letter from Sulieman Nassif to the editor of "Al Kawkab," Cairo
Copy of translation of Grand Mufti's speech to American Red Cross Committee [printed]
Copy of letter from President Wilson to Rabbi Wise
"The Situation in Palestine," copy of memorandum
Notes on Palestinian Greek Orthodox Community, prepared for W. Y. by brother of the Dragoman of the Russian Consulate
Copy of [anti-Zionist] address by Preacher in the Mosque of Omar Mohammed El Afifi

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Treaty between Feisal and Weizmann, and Zionist Proposals for the Peace Conference
Copy of a petition from inhabitants of El-Yamoun to French Consulate at St. Jean, [in French]
"Palestine's Progress," from the Egyptian Gazette
"Jew, Arab and Armenian: The Scope of Zionism," clipping from Egyptian Gazette
Copy of letter from Faisal to Felix Frankfurter [in French]
Copy of letter from Feisal to F. Frankfurter [extract from the "Jewish Chronicle"]
Copy of petition from 421 notables of Nablus to U. S. Diplomatic Agency, Cairo
Notes by William Yale on a talk with M. Andre Spire
Copy of letter from William H. Taft to Isaac Landman
"Statement of policy on the Palestine question," (Joint Foreign Committee of the Jewish Board of Deputies and the Anglo - Jewish Association)
Copy of record of conversation between H. Morgenthau, Lucien Wolf, M. Bogart, and Isaac Landman, Paris
Miscellaneous Materials on Syria
Brochure prepared by Dr. Howard Bliss (American College, Beyrouth) on "The Right of the People of Syria...etc." [French and English texts]
Copy of letter from Faisal to Lloyd George
Copy of letter from Faris S. Nimr, editor of Al-Mokattan , dealing with Syrian political aspirations, to Mr. Gary, American diplomatic agent, Cairo.
Copy of "Confidential Notes" on French propaganda agents, n.a.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Copy of French proposal for the administration of Syria (38 pages) ["Note sur l'organization générale de l'administration de la nouvelle Syrie"]
"British commitments to King Hussein," 2 copies of memorandum, n.a.
"Preserving the open door to the oil fields of the world," n.a.

Copy of memorandum on St. Jean de Maurienne Agreement
Copy of notes on Shereef Ali Haidar, n.a.
"Suggestions for Syrian Mandate" by Ribahni[?]
Copies of two Tahet articles on the Syrian problem
Copy of letter to President Wilson from Syrian notables and officers [in French]

Copy of Aide Memoire on the Sherif of Mecca's revolt, 1916 June, Arab Bureau, Savoy Hotel, Cairo
Copy of letter of Sir Mark Sykes to [?], .
Copy of an open letter addressed to a correspondent in El Kobla , by Hakki Bey el Am of Damascus, Cairo

Aperçu Historique stir l'Alliance Libanaise [with English translation]
"Assyrians," by Abraham Yohannon
Copies of miscellaneous papers in re Taking of Beyrouth
Byrne, E. H., "Report on the Desires of the Syrians"
Copy of "Note [sur] les évenements de Syrie" [in French], Beyrouth
Copy of text of Anglo-French Declaration on Syria; published in "Palestine News," by direction of General Allenby

Miscellaneous Statistics on Protestant Missionary Stations in Arabia
"The Ottoman Caliphate," translation of 6 chapters from "Appunti sulla natura... [etc.]" by Carlo Alfonso Nallino...[etc.]
"The Bedouins and their politics" by G. Bie-Ravndal [Consul-General, Beirut]
List of Arab Personalities on Mesopotamia

Mesopotamian Committees: Petitions to President Wilson [French and English]; Damascus

Miscellaneous papers from Comité Libanais de Paris

"The Dismemberment of the Ottoman Empire"

did.unittitle: "Arab Independence," copy of report of a British intelligence agent [copy sent to Peace Commission, Paris] [in French]
Copy of letter from Hedjaz Delegates to Colonel House, Paris (annotated)
Copy of translation of what is reported to be a treaty between Emir Faisal and Mustapha Kemal. ["Probably false" - William Yale] [in French]
Copy of report on "The Apparent Situation in Syria, July 1, 1919," by A. H. Lybyer
"Report on Syria, Palestine and Mt. Lebanon for the American Commissioners" [Constantinople] (2 copies). Includes revisions made in 1928.
Map showing proposed division of Ottoman Empire (to accompany report of American Commission on Mandates in Turkey). One of the original MS copies.
Copy of the "Report upon the non-Arabic-Speaking Portions of the Former Ottoman Empire by the American Section of the International Commission on Mandates in Turkey"
Copy of the "Report upon Mesopotamia by the American section of the International Commission on Mandates in Turkey"
Copy of the "Report upon Syria by the American Section of the International Commission on Mandates in Turkey"
Copies of table of contents and summary of King-Crane Report. Includes autograph signatures.
Copy of memorandum re: Emir Faisal's communication to Lloyd George
Copy of Aide-Memoire Lloyd George to Polk re: occupation of Syria
Copy of reports-in-detail on "Position of Syrian Question"
Copy of report on "Clemenceau - Lloyd George Agreement on Syria"
Miscellaneous Items
Miscellaneous printed items
2 maps of Syria [in French]
Article: "Bref Aperçu sur l'Effort Arabe," n.a.
Copy of a "Report on Syria," by George R. Montgomery
"Report on the Proposals for an Independent Arab State or States," by Howard Crosby Butler
"Preliminary Report on Land in the Ottoman Empire" (outline)
"Report of the Principal Highways of Syria," prepared by the British Military Authorities for the Expedition in Palestine and Syria. [Copy sent to Second Section, G. S., G. H. G., A. E. F.]
"Report upon Just and Practical Boundaries for Sub-divisions of the Turkish Empire," by W. L. Westermann and others
Copy of William Yale's article on "Ambassador Henry Morgenthau's 'Special Mission' of 1917"
